style: USENIX systems paper figure. White background, sans-serif, restrained colour palette. Three panels arranged left-to-right under a single shared baseline, each labelled with a step index (t_0, t_1, t_2). Each panel shows the prompt as a strip of token-block rectangles, plus one terse caption and one one-line technical note in monospace below.
title: Lifecycle of a tool observation under KV-pointer recall.
subtitle: The observation's KV state is captured to a side cache on compaction (t_1) and restored to its original logical positions on recall (t_2); the prefix cache is preserved across both transitions.

panels.label: t_0
panels.caption: Steady state. The observation is in the prompt; its KV blocks are resident in the engine's paged KV cache and indexed by the prefix-cache hash table.
panels.tech_note: obs KV in paged GPU blocks; chain hash H_obs entries point to those blocks.

panels.label: t_1
panels.caption: Compaction. The renderer substitutes a fixed-size placeholder for the observation. The observation's KV blocks are copied to a content-addressed CPU pinned side cache and released; the suffix's KV blocks remain physically resident.
panels.tech_note: side cache: obs_id (content hash) -> per-layer K/V on pinned host memory. Released blocks fall to the engine's LRU free queue.

panels.label: t_2
panels.caption: Recall. The renderer re-emits the observation at its original logical positions. The scheduler allocates fresh blocks, copies the saved KV from the side cache, rotates the suffix's K vectors in place to compensate for the position shift, and registers the existing suffix blocks under the recall-prompt's chain hash.
panels.tech_note: splice + rotate-by-Δ + auxiliary chain-hash insert. Rotation reuses the engine's rotary kernel via R(p+Δ) = R(Δ)·R(p). Hash table insertion is append-only; existing entries are not invalidated.

invariants_callout: The observation's KV is captured, not recomputed.
Suffix KV blocks are physically retained across the compacted period.
Cache entries are append-only: keys are added on recall, never removed.
